content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
October 12, 1989 
Harvey Tsuboi, Program Technician III 
Political Reform Division 
Secretary of State 
P.O. Box 1467 
Sacramento, CA 
95807 
Dear Mr. Tsuboi: 
Re: Your Request for Informal 
Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-531 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice 
provided to you concerning the lobbying disclosure provisions 
of the Political Reform Act. 1 
Your letter, dated September 8, 1989, correctly states 
that when the lobbying firm Douglas, Golden and Associates 
only monitors legislation and administrative action on behalf 
of a client, the client is not required to be registered or 
file lobbyist employer reports, and the firm is not required 
to disclose payments received from the client. 
However, if a client expressly or implicitly authorizes 
the lobbying firm to engage in direct communication with 
elective state officials, legislative officials or agency 
officials for the purpose of influencing or attempting to 
influence legislative or administrative action, the client 
becomes a lobbyist employer and both the client and the 
lobbying firm must disclose payments for such activities. 
1 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Please note that initiative-related and legislative-
related services must be reported retroactively if, within 
one year the client authorizes the lobbying firm to 
communicate directly with elected state officials, 
legislative officials or agency officials for the purpose of 
influencing action on the same or substantially the same 
matter. 
(Sections 86100-86118, Regulation 18614.) 
I hope this satisfactorily responds to your request. 
A 
copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience. 
Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have 
additional questions. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
Gen:~. 
nsel 4 d 
7.~~<<'~ 
By. 
Lynda Cassady 
Political Reform Consultant 
Enclosure 


L 
March Fong Eu 
Secretary of State 
September 8, 1989 
Ms. MaryAnn Kvasager 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 'J' Street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Ms. Kvasager: 
1230 J Street 
P.o. Box 1467 
Sacramento, California 95807 
POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION 
(916) 322-4880 
FPPC 
SEP 111989 
This letter is to clarify advice we have received regarding the filing of obligations 
of Douglas, Golden and Associates; Southwest Gas Corporation; and California State 
Management Association. 
While the firm Douglas, Golden and Associates has an active client, California State 
Employees' Association, it merely monitors legislation for Southwest Gas Corporation 
and California State Management Association. 
It . is our understanding that as long as 
the firm's services are limited to monitoring legislation, the firm is not required 
to register either of the clients with our office, nor would the clients have a 
responsibility to file any reports with us. 
If Douglas', Golden and Associates begins 
lobbying on behalf of their clients, the firm would then need to file a Form 605 with 
appropriate Forms 602. 
After which time, the firm would list Southwest Gas 
Corporation and/or California Statement Management Association on their quarterly 
reports and the clients themselves would begin submitting quarterly Forms 635. 
Please either confirm or correct this understanding in writing so we may make it part 
of our records. 
Thank you, 
• 
\\~v.-... -C\,.~ 
HARVEY TSUBOI, Program Technician III 
Political Reform Division

If Douglas, Golden and Associates begins

appropriate Forms 602. 
After which time, the firm would list Southwest Gas

Please either confirm or correct this understanding in writing so we may make it part

Thank you,

HARVEY TSUBOI, Program 
III 
Political Reform Division 
:mw 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
September 15, 1989 
Harvie Tsuboi 
Secretary of State's Office 
Political Reform Division 
P.O. Box 1467 
Sacramento, CA 
95807 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-531 
Dear Mr. Tsuboi: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on September 11, 1989. 
Your letter 
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact 
that division directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely, 
1:'IJ.lZL~ t ,~~~ 
Kathryn ~. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor seeks confirmation whether a lobbying firm (Douglas, Golden and Associates) that merely monitors legislation for certain clients must register those clients or file reports, and what obligations arise if the firm begins direct lobbying activities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Monitoring legislation alone does not require client registration or reporting; however, when a client authorizes direct communication with officials to influence legislative or administrative action, both the client and firm must disclose payments. Prior monitoring services must be reported retroactively if direct lobbying on the same matter is authorized within one year.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Clear advice letter responding to Secretary of State's inquiry. Contains both incoming request and FPPC response. QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections clearly delineated. OCR quality is good with minor artifacts.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: While the firm Douglas, Golden and Associates has an active client, California State Employees' Association, it merely monitors legislation for Southwest Gas Corporation and California State Management Association. It is our understanding that as long as the firm's services are limited to monitoring legislation, the firm is not required to register either of the clients with our office, nor would the clients have a responsibility to file any reports with us. If Douglas, Golden and Associates begins lobbying on behalf of their clients, the firm would then need to file a Form 605 with appropriate Forms 602. Please either confirm or correct this understanding in writing so we may make it part of our records.

CONCLUSION: Your letter, dated September 8, 1989, correctly states that when the lobbying firm Douglas, Golden and Associates only monitors legislation and administrative action on behalf of a client, the client is not required to be registered or file lobbyist employer reports, and the firm is not required to disclose payments received from the client. However, if a client expressly or implicitly authorizes the lobbying firm to engage in direct communication with elective state officials, legislative officials or agency officials for the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence legislative or administrative action, the client becomes a lobbyist employer and both the client and the lobbying firm must disclose payments for such activities. Please note that initiative-related and legislative-related services must be reported retroactively if, within one year the client authorizes the lobbying firm to communicate directly with elected state officials, legislative officials or agency officials for the purpose of influencing action on the same or substantially the same matter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms that a lobbying firm performing only legislative monitoring services need not register clients or file reports, but when clients authorize direct communication with officials to influence legislative action, registration and disclosure requirements apply. Retroactive reporting is required for prior monitoring services if direct lobbying on the same matter begins within one year.
